name: Georgian Potato Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of heavy cream, cottage cheese, and flour. Mix until smooth, then blend in the remaining cream. This can be done by hand or with a mixer.
2. Set aside in the refrigerator.
3. Divide the spices into two equal portions.
4. Place a large, heavy pot on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
5. Add all vegetables except for 2 cups of diced potatoes and one spice portion to the pot. Stir and cook over high heat for 4-6 minutes, scraping the crusts that form on the bottom of the pot.
6. Add apple juice and then apple sauce. Stir and cook for 2-3 minutes.
7. Add stock and the other spice portion. Cook while stirring for 12 minutes.
8. Remove the mixture (either rice it or puree in a blender until smooth) and return to the pot.
9. Add the 2 cups of diced potatoes, bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, stir well, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
10. If more liquid is needed, add stock. Blend in the cream mixture and continue cooking while stirring for 3-4 minutes.
